concepts.title: India's Export Orientation
concepts.description: India has shifted from import substitution to export orientation since 1991 due to liberalization policies.

concepts.title: Major Export Commodities
concepts.description: Textiles, engineering products, gems and jewellery, chemicals, leather products, and pharmaceuticals are the major export commodities of India.

concepts.title: Types of Trade
concepts.description: India engages in intra-regional, inter-regional, and international trade, with a focus on the latter due to global market opportunities.

concepts.title: India's Export Markets
concepts.description: MENRAG (Middle East, North Africa, Russia, Asia, and Gulf) countries are the major export markets for India, but the USA is also a significant market.

concepts.title: India's Import-Export Balance
concepts.description: Students often forget that India has a trade deficit, meaning its imports exceed its exports. This is due to the country's high demand for oil and capital goods.

concepts.title: India's Export Growth
concepts.description: India's export growth has been steady, with an average annual growth rate of 15% since 1991, although this rate has declined in recent years.

concepts.title: India's Major Trading Partners
concepts.description: The USA, China, the UAE, and the UK are India's major trading partners, with the USA being the largest trading partner.

concepts.title: India's Export Promotion Policies
concepts.description: India has implemented various export promotion policies, including the Export Promotion Councils, the Export-Import Policy, and the Foreign Trade Policy, to boost its exports.
